title: Grove Claim
description: A mutable coordination object for live work in an agent swarm. Claims prevent duplicate work by enforcing at most one active claim per target_ref. They are the ONLY mutable objects in the Grove protocol — all other objects (contributions, relations, artifacts) are immutable.

claim_id.description: Unique identifier for this claim. Assigned by the claiming agent or orchestrator.

target_ref.description: What is being claimed. This is a free-form reference — it may be a contribution CID, a task description, or a structured ref like 'task-1/implementation'. At most one active claim may exist per target_ref value.

intent_summary.description: Human/agent-readable description of what the agent intends to do with this claim.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp of when the claim was first created. Immutable — never changes after creation. Distinct from heartbeat_at, which tracks liveness.

heartbeat_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp of the last heartbeat. Updated on each keepalive to prove the agent is still alive and working. Initially set to the same value as created_at.

lease_expires_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC 3339 timestamp when this claim auto-expires if no heartbeat is received. Extended on each heartbeat by the lease duration.

context.description: Optional machine-readable metadata for this claim. Use for structured coordination data such as branch/commit being worked on, resource allocations, parent workflow identifiers, or priority indicators.

agent_identity.description: Identity metadata for the agent that created this claim.

agent_id.description: Stable machine-readable identifier for the agent. Unlike agent_name (which is human-readable and may change), agent_id should be a persistent, unique identifier used for attribution and query filtering.

model.description: The model used by the agent (e.g., 'claude-opus-4-6', 'o3').

version.description: Version identifier for the agent or its configuration.
